enter.speaker: narrator
enter.text: A woman stands at the dock's edge, arms crossed, watching the water the way a soldier watches a gate. She does not turn as you approach. She already knows you are there.

nessa-look.speaker: nessa
nessa-look.text: The docks are not for tourists. But you do not look like a tourist. You look like trouble. Good. We need trouble.

whats-wrong.speaker: nessa
whats-wrong.text: The fish stopped coming three days ago. The water turned darker. Something is moving down there — slow, heavy, patient. We lost three boats this month. The nets come up torn, or not at all.

help-offer.speaker: nessa
help-offer.text: Help. Yes. I have been waiting for someone willing to say that word. The deep water off the end dock — things have been surfacing. Strange things. Fish up three of them for me. I need to know what we are dealing with.

help-accept.speaker: nessa
help-accept.text: Use the fishing spots at the ends of the docks. Whatever you pull up, bring it to me. I will be here. I am always here.

village-lore.speaker: nessa
village-lore.text: Duskmere was built on old foundations. The first settlers found cut stone under the mud — doorways, stair wells, things that should not be this far from any city. They built over it. People always build over what they do not understand.

village-lore-2.speaker: nessa
village-lore-2.text: My grandmother said the lake was not always here. That it filled in one night, swallowed whatever was below. I used to think she was trying to frighten me. Now I am not certain.

leave.speaker: nessa
leave.text: Mind the water. And if you see something move beneath it — do not look twice.
